mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which weakens consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment rate declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate weakening supply-side capacity and demand, which predict negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 20d returns in combined decline scenarios are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) during high inflation environments predicts equity market overvaluation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of high-inflation, rising-spending scenarios, or CPIAUCSL declines without corresponding equity losses.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 1% signals strengthening manufacturing activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 for any 1-year window, or 5d returns in high-industrial-growth scenarios are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with elevated federal funds rates predicts monetary tightening pressures, which lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with rate hikes
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances with both high employment and elevated rates, or interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 5%) predict reduced liquidity and higher discount rates, which drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of high-rate environments, or FEDFUNDS declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate improving consumer confidence and demand, which predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.04 for any 3-year window, or 60d returns in rising-housing scenarios are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).
